big_lotto.note: P246K canonical NIST audit is GREEN on 2,113 canonical rows; this is a data-quality result, not a strategy signal.

daily_539.note: P230C closed the prior survivor; no active candidate remains.

power_lotto.note: P231B first-zone backward-OOS dry-run was NULL; any new hypothesis would require fresh pre-registration.

star_3_4.note: P227C box-play was underpowered; P214C straight-play was NULL and positional order is lost in sorted storage.

research_state.inventory_note: Current registry is the live SSOT; P232A historical scoreboard is retained as a replay snapshot and is reconciled here rather than used as the sole current-state source.

candidate_next_directions.title: Cross-lottery null/positive evidence dashboard
candidate_next_directions.value: High — one place to compare active / rejected / retired / artifact-only rows, replay coverage, and historical classifications.
candidate_next_directions.risk: Low — read-only reporting and view composition only.
candidate_next_directions.urgency: Medium — the current inventory spans multiple lifecycle labels and benefits from a single honest view.
candidate_next_directions.prerequisites: Read-only access to current registry + replay snapshot; no DB write.

candidate_next_directions.title: Replay/catalog lifecycle badge and filter refresh
candidate_next_directions.value: High — makes lifecycle visible without hiding historical replay rows.
candidate_next_directions.risk: Low-medium — UI/API behavior change only if surfaced to users.
candidate_next_directions.urgency: Medium — current state already contains active, rejected, retired, observation, and artifact-only entries.
candidate_next_directions.prerequisites: Define the exact badge vocabulary and keep historical rows visible by default.

candidate_next_directions.title: Canonical replay refresh and stale-label cleanup
candidate_next_directions.value: Medium-high — rebuild the current scoreboard from the current registry so the historical snapshot is not mistaken for live state.
candidate_next_directions.risk: Low — read-only unless a new artifact is written.
candidate_next_directions.urgency: Medium — the 20260604 scoreboard is historically useful but stale relative to the current registry.
candidate_next_directions.prerequisites: Use the current registry as SSOT; keep P232A as historical evidence only.

candidate_next_directions.title: Raw history add-on labeling for user-facing views
candidate_next_directions.value: Medium — clarifies the BIG_LOTTO raw/canonical split and similar label semantics.
candidate_next_directions.urgency: Low-medium — useful for truthfulness, but not required for current research governance.
candidate_next_directions.prerequisites: Frontend/API authorization and explicit display contract.

candidate_next_directions.title: Archived script reactivation / migration only when a script is revived
candidate_next_directions.value: Low-medium — keeps dormant code aligned with canonical helpers when reactivated.
candidate_next_directions.risk: Low — narrow code migration, but only worth doing when a script is truly in use again.
candidate_next_directions.urgency: Low — no active deployable candidate depends on it today.
candidate_next_directions.prerequisites: Explicit reactivation decision for the specific archived script.

candidate_next_directions.title: Annotation table Type D only if a downstream consumer appears
candidate_next_directions.value: Low-medium — would label row families without mutating strategy logic.
candidate_next_directions.risk: Medium — requires a controlled DB write and backup discipline.
candidate_next_directions.urgency: Low — the current inventory does not require it.
candidate_next_directions.prerequisites: Explicit Type D authorization and a concrete consumer need.
